LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT PROVIDING THAT DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY BEAUTY PRODUCTS INC. SHALL ONLY MANUFACTURE FOR SALE, IMPORT FOR SALE, SELL OR DISTRIBUTE FOR SALE, IN OR INTO CALIFORNIA, REFORMULATED PRODUCTS COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Plaintiff John Moore's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the injunctive relief that is required by the settlement providing that Defendant University Beauty Products Inc. shall only manufacture for sale, import for sale, sell or distribute for sale, in or into California, reformulated products complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
